results.title: Oil prices rise after new Middle East attacks
results.description: On Monday morning, the price of a barrel of North Sea oil rose by over three percent to 94 dollars. The increase follows new attacks in the Middle East, which according to Reuters challenge optimism about a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, where about 20 percent of the world’s oil passes. Since Israel and [...]

results.title: Couple stabbed to death while sleeping
results.description: VILLASIS, Pangasinan – A couple was stabbed to death in Barangay Piaz here on Sunday night, May 31. Initial investigation said the victims – a 40-year-old female Overseas Filipino Worker and a 42-year-old male farmer – were sleeping when the suspect, a 47-year-old male farmer and former live-in partner of the female victim, appeared. An 18-year-old student and a 48-year-old housekeeper were awakened by loud screams and saw the suspect stabbing the victims before fleeing through the back door with a bladed weapon used in the attack. The victims sustained multiple stab wounds and declared dead in a hospital. Police are conducting follow-up investigation.

results.title: Arm, IBM and Hewlett Packard soar as Nvidia chip ‘reinvention’ extends software rally
results.description: Software stocks spiked ahead of Monday’s opening bell after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled plans for a new chip that will power personal computers. Investors are piling into a range of computer-related names on the first trading day of June, with ServiceNow leading the premarket rise after soaring 14.4% ahead of the opening bell. results.title: Why is market falling today? Sensex drops 600 points, Nifty below 23,400: 4 key factors
results.description: Indian equity markets erased early gains and slipped sharply into the red on Monday as escalating Iran-US tensions, rising crude oil prices, aggressive FII selling and higher US bond yields weighed on sentiment. While IT stocks outperformed and cushioned some losses, broader markets remained under pressure, with investors closely watching key technical support levels on the Nifty.

results.description: The government had provided an eight-month window for beneficiaries to complete their e-KYC

results.title: Indian care worker wins lost wages after UK sponsor failed to provide work
results.description: An Indian care worker, Shabin Shaji, has won over £28,000 in lost wages and holiday pay after his sponsoring company, Swan Care Solutions Ltd, failed to provide him with work. The tribunal found Shaji was employed but prevented from working by the company, constituting an unauthorised deduction from his wages.
